name: Blue Beam Project
description: The Blue Beam Project is a conspiracy theory claiming that NASA, with the help of the United Nations, is attempting to implement a New World Order by staging a technologically simulated 'Second Coming.' The theory alleges that this will involve using advanced holographic projections and sound technology to create artificial religious manifestations in the sky, designed to deceive the masses into accepting a unified global religion. It originated in 1994 with Serge Monast, a Canadian journalist known for his anti-New World Order views. Monast claimed that the project would involve a staged alien invasion and the manipulation of natural disasters to instill fear and chaos. Proponents cite unexplained sonic booms and holographic-like phenomena as potential evidence. Mainstream perspectives dismiss the Blue Beam Project as pseudoscience and point to the lack of verifiable evidence, the implausibility of such a complex and coordinated global conspiracy, and the reliance on misinterpretations of scientific and technological advancements. Critics highlight the absence of credible scientific support and the reliance on anecdotal evidence and unsubstantiated claims. The theory has gained traction within various conspiracy circles and is often linked to other New World Order narratives.
subtitle: Holographic deception for global control?

claims.claim: Holographic projections will simulate a Second Coming.
claims.evidence: Reports of unusual aerial phenomena and claims of advanced holographic technology.
claims.criticism: No verifiable evidence of such technology being deployed on a global scale. Holographic projections are limited by line of sight and atmospheric conditions.

claims.claim: NASA and the UN are orchestrating the project to establish a New World Order.
claims.evidence: Allegations of secret meetings and hidden agendas within these organizations.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence linking NASA or the UN to such a conspiracy. Openness and transparency initiatives within these organizations contradict the secrecy required for such a project.

claims.claim: Staged alien invasion to unite humanity under a single government.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of government documents and sightings of unidentified flying objects.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence of an alien invasion being staged. UFO sightings are often misidentified natural phenomena or military experiments.

keyFigures.name: Serge Monast
keyFigures.role: Originator of the Blue Beam Project theory

keyFigures.name: David Icke
keyFigures.role: Prominent conspiracy theorist who popularized the theory

keyFigures.name: Mick West
keyFigures.role: Debunker of conspiracy theories, including the Blue Beam Project

timeline.event: The theory gains traction online and within conspiracy communities
The theory continues to circulate, often linked to other conspiracy narratives

sources.author: Mick West
sources.work: Escaping the Rabbit Hole: How to Debunk Conspiracy Theories Using Facts, Logic, and Respect
